dn20:1.2: I have heard that on one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling among the Sakyans at Kapilavatthu in the Great Forest, together with a large Saṅgha of approximately five hundred monks, all of them arahants.
dn20:1.3: And most of the devatās from ten world-systems had gathered in order to see the Blessed One & the Saṅgha of monks.
dn20:2.1: Then the thought occurred to four devatās of the ranks from the Pure Abodes:
dn20:2.2: “The Blessed One is dwelling among the Sakyans at Kapilavatthu in the Great Forest, together with a large Saṅgha of about five hundred monks, all of them arahants.
dn20:2.3: And most of the devatās from ten world-systems have gathered in order to see the Blessed One & the Saṅgha of monks.
dn20:2.4: Let us also approach the Blessed One and, on arrival, let us each speak a verse in his presence.”
dn20:3.1: Then, just as a strong man might extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm, those devatās disappeared from among the Devas of the Pure Abodes and reappeared before the Blessed One.
dn20:3.2: Having paid homage to him, they stood to one side.
dn20:3.3: As they were standing there, one devatā recited this verse in the Blessed One’s presence:
dn20:3.6: We have come to this Dhamma meeting
dn20:3.8: Then another devatā recited this verse in the Blessed One’s presence:
dn20:3.11: Like a charioteer holding the reins,
dn20:3.12: the wise ones guard their faculties.”
dn20:3.13: Then another devatā recited this verse in the Blessed One’s presence:
dn20:3.14: “Having cut through barrenness, cut the cross-bar,
dn20:3.17: young nāgas well tamed by the One with Eyes.”
dn20:3.18: Then another devatā recited this verse in the Blessed One’s presence:
dn20:3.19: “Those who have gone to the Buddha for refuge
dn20:3.20: will not go to the plane of woe.
dn20:3.22: they will fill the hosts of the devas.”
dn20:4.1: Then the Blessed One addressed the monks:
dn20:4.2: “Monks, most of the devatās from ten world-systems have gathered in order to see the Tathāgata & the Saṅgha of monks.
dn20:4.3: Those who, in the past, were Worthy Ones, Rightly Self-awakened, at most had their devatā-gathering like mine at the present.
dn20:4.4: Those who, in the future, will be Worthy Ones, Rightly Self-awakened, will at most have their devatā-gathering like mine at the present.
dn20:4.5: I will tell you the names of the deva hosts.
dn20:4.6: I will describe to you the names of the deva hosts.
dn20:4.7: I will teach you the names of the deva hosts.
dn20:4.8: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.”
dn20:4.9: “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
dn20:5.1: I will recite a verse of tribute.
dn20:5.2: Those who live where spirits dwell,
dn20:5.9: Knowing that more than 500 of them
dn20:5.10: had come to the forest of Kapilavatthu,
dn20:5.11: the Teacher then said to them,
dn20:6.2: Knowledge appeared to them, vision of non-human beings.
dn20:6.7: Some gained vision of innumerable devas
dn20:6.10: the One-with-Vision felt moved to speak.
dn20:6.11: The Teacher then said to them,
dn20:8.12: He, too, has come to the forest meeting.
dn20:9.1: And Dhataraṭṭha, who rules as king of the Eastern Direction,
dn20:9.4: A glorious, great king is he,
dn20:9.11: And Virūḷha, who rules as king of the Southern Direction,
dn20:9.14: A glorious, great king is he,
dn20:9.21: And Virūpakkha, who rules as king of the Western Direction,
dn20:9.23: as lord of the nāgas: A
dn20:9.31: And Kuvera, who rules as king of the Northern Direction,
dn20:9.34: A glorious, great king is he,
dn20:10.1: Their deceitful vassals have also come
dn20:10.10: King Naḷa, the Bull of the People,
dn20:11.1: Then there have also come nāgas
dn20:11.8: He, too, has come to the forest meeting.
dn20:11.9: They who swoop down swiftly on nāga kings,
dn20:11.10: divine, twice-born, winged, their eyesight pure:
dn20:11.11: (Garuḍas) came from the sky to the midst of the forest.
dn20:11.13: But the Buddha made the nāga kings safe,
dn20:11.15: Addressing one another with affectionate words,
dn20:11.16: the nāgas & Supaṇṇas made the Buddha their refuge.
dn20:12.1: Defeated by Indra of the thunderbolt hand,
dn20:13.4: Soma together with Yasa, the prestigious devas of the hosts
dn20:14.3: the devas dependent on the moon
dn20:14.5: The devas dependent on the sun
dn20:14.8: and the sprites of the clouds have come.
dn20:16.3: the devas corrupted by play have come,
dn20:16.4: as well as devas corrupted by mind.
dn20:17.3: headed by devas totally in white.
dn20:17.8: who brings rain to the lands:
dn20:19.4: together with others in like manner (thinking:)
dn20:19.5: “We’ll see the one who has transcended birth,
dn20:19.6: who has no bounds, who has crossed over the flood, effluent-free,
dn20:19.7: the Mighty One, crossing over the flood,
dn20:19.8: like the moon emerging from the dark fortnight.”
dn20:20.2: together with sons of the Powerful One,
dn20:20.4: They too have come to the forest meeting.
dn20:20.5: Great Brahmā, who stands over 1,000 Brahmā worlds,
dn20:20.8: Prestigious is he, with a terrifying body.
dn20:20.10: each the lord of his own realm—
dn20:20.11: and in their midst has come
dn20:21.2: When all these devas with Indras & Brahmās had come,
dn20:21.4: Now look at the Dark One’s foolishness!
dn20:21.8: Don’t let anyone at all escape!”
dn20:21.11: slapping the ground with his hand,
dn20:21.12: making a horrendous din, as when
dn20:21.13: a storm cloud bursts with thunder,
dn20:22.2: the One-with-Vision felt moved to speak.
dn20:22.3: The Teacher then said to them,
dn20:22.7: Listening to the Awakened One’s instruction,
dn20:22.9: The army retreated from those without passion,
dn20:22.10: without raising even a hair on their bodies.
dn20:22.14: disciples outstanding among the human race.
